questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. artificial and highly elaborate way of writing or speaking
questions.explanation: correct answer is “euphuism”.Euphuism – artificial and highly elaborate way of writing or speaking.Bibliography – collection of names of books useful for the purpose.Lexicography – one who complies dictionary.Verbose – containing more words than necessary.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of “stupendous”.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “Marvelous”.Stupendous – causing astonishment or wonder.Marvelous – very good, wonderful.Terrible – very unpleasant, causing great shock or injury.Magnifying – to make something look bigger than it is.Tedious – boring and lasting for a long time.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with an appropriate synonym of the word mentioned in the bracket. The novel was filled with ________ (Enigmatic) characters.
questions.explanation: correct answer “mysterious”.Explanation:Enigmatic -mysterious and impossible to understand completely.Mysterious – same meaning as enigmatic.Unambiguous – expressed in a way that makes it completely clear what is meant.Understandable - that can be understand.Lucid – clear and easy to understand.

questions.question: Parts of the given sentence have been given as options. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. The principal have ordered that the teachers should complete the assignment immediately.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “The principal have ordered”.“Have” used with the pronouns I, you, we, and they.“Has” used with the pronouns He, she, it and other names or designations.The correct sentence would be: The principal has ordered that the teachers should complete the assignment immediately.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “Hygeine”.Explanation:Correct spelling is Hygiene which means “keeping yourself and things around you clean, in order to prevent disease”.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the highlighted part of the given sentence. Mahes does not helping to getting angry when he sees someone being treated unfairly.
options: may not help to getting angry
will not help to gets angry
was not help to being getting angry
questions.explanation: correct answer is “cannot help getting angry”.Explanation:In option A: this option doesn’t grammatically correct. The use of “may not help” is correct, but “getting angry” should not follow the preposition “to”.In option B: this option is grammatically correct. In option C: this option doesn’t grammatically correct. According to this option there is error in tense “gets” should be in the base form “get” to match the subject. Hence “Will not help to gets angry” doesn’t correct. In option D: this option contains incorrect verb. The past tense “was not” fit in the given sentence. Hence this option doesn’t correct grammatically.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word His boastful nature made all the difference.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “cocky”Explanation:In the given question we talk about “his boastful nature made all the difference”. So, the meaning of boastful is “showing too much pride”.Courageous (adj): having or showing courage.Modest (adj): not usually talking about or making obvious your own abilities and achievements.Horrific (adj): very bad and shocking.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who dance around excitedly
questions.explanation: correct answer is “Cavort”.Explanation:Cavort – one who dance around excitedly.Excited – feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm.Ecstatic – extremely happy.Stalwart – always loyal to the same organization, team, etc.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. If tina are going out surely, we will follow her.
questions.explanation: correct answer “is going out”.Explanation: According to subject verb agreement verb agrees with the subject. When the subject is singular’ the verb should also be in the singular form. ‘Tina’ is a singular subject, so the verb “are” should be changed into “is” (singular form).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Cliff hanger
options: someone who hangs on cliffs for recreational purposes.
A dangerous or risky activity involving cliffs.
A suspenseful or exciting situation with an uncertain outcome.
A person who enjoys climbing mountains.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “A suspenseful or exciting situation with an uncertain outcome”.Cliff hanger – A contest whose outcome is in doubt up to the very end.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The old woman was helped by the police while crossing the road
options: The police was helped the old woman while crossing the road.
The police helped the old woman while crossing the road.
The police has helped the old woman while crossing the road.
The police is helping the old woman while crossing the road.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “The police helped the old woman while crossing the road”.Explanation: General formula of simple past passive voice – object + was/were + V3 + by + subjectThe active voice of “The old woman was helped by the police while crossing the road”will be “The police helped the old woman while crossing the road.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. For the next two years the Franks and another family lived cloistered in the ‘Secret Annex’ of an old office building. B. In 1942, with the Nazis occupying Holland, a thirteen-year-old Jewish girl, Anne, and her family fled their home in Amsterdam and went into hiding. C. In her diary Anne Frank recorded vivid impressions of these experiences during this period. D. Cut off from the outside world, they faced hunger, boredom, the constant cruelties of living in confined quarters, and the ever-present threat of discovery and death.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “BADC”Correct sequence:B. In 1942, with the Nazis occupying Holland, a thirteen-year-old Jewish girl, Anne, and her family fled their home in Amsterdam and went into hiding.A. For the next two years the Franks and another family lived cloistered in the ‘Secret Annex’ of an old office building.D. Cut off from the outside world, they faced hunger, boredom, the constant cruelties of living in confined quarters, and the ever-present threat of discovery and death.C. In her diary Anne Frank recorded vivid impressions of these experiences during this period.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an INCORRECTLY spelt word. It was really surprising / that the four bosom friends fought / over such a triviale matter / and stopped all communications with each other.
options: and stopped all communications with each other
that the two bosom friends fought
questions.explanation: correct answer is “over such a triviale matter”. Correct spelling – Trivial.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The delicious dish was eaten by the jury members.
options: The jury members were eating the delicious dish.
The jury members was eating the delicious dish.
The jury members are eating the delicious dish.
The jury members ate the delicious dish.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “The jury members ate the delicious dish”.Explanation: Rule of Simple past tense Active voice – subject + V2 + object Passive voice – object + was/ were + V3 + by + subjectHence, “The jury members ate the delicious dish” is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Out of thin air
questions.explanation: correct answer is “not expected or planned”.Meaning of out of thin air – it is used to say that someone or something appears in a sudden and unexpected way.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. before going for trip you should Tick all the boxes.
options: To check the lock of the house
questions.explanation: correct answer is “To fulfill the required norms”.Meaning of the given idiom – fulfill all the necessary requirements.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Buoyant
questions.explanation: correct answer is “Abstain”.Explanation: Buoyant – happy and confident.Satisfy – to make somebody pleased by doing or giving him what he wantsSatiate – to completely satisfy a desire or need.Abstain – to stop from doing something.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Shyam has written many books.
options: Many books were being written by her.
Many books have been written by shyam.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “many books have been written by shyam”.Rule: active voice – subject + has/have + V3 + object Passive voice – object + has/ have + been + V3 + by + subjectShyam has written many books converted into Many books have been written by shyam.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Sunita always reads / the instructions / careful while / attempting the paper.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is “careful while”The word “careful” is grammatically incorrect.“Careful” is an adjective.“Carefully” is an adverb of manner.In this sentence adverb of manner is used. So, the correct answer would be “Sunita always reads the instructions carefully while attempting the paper”.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “copuliate”.Copulate means to engage in sexual intercourse.Horticulture means the art or practice of garden cultivation and management.Portion means an individual’s part or share of something.Ignition means the electrical system in an engine that causes the fuel to burn or explore in order to start the engine.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When trees are illegally or irresponsibly cut, forest ecosystems are (1)______. Their ability to store carbon, shelter wildlife, filter water, and to provide food, products, and jobs for (2)______ is compromised. Global forest loss also leads to more greenhouse gases in the (3)______. This is much more than what comes from all cars, trucks, trains, planes and ships in the world. But when forests thrive, they absorb carbon dioxide and (4)______ down climate change. Protecting forests does much more than just keep trees standing because forests are the (5)______ of the planet and serve as the home, market and apothecary of people living on it.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “weakened”.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When trees are illegally or irresponsibly cut, forest ecosystems are (1)______. Their ability to store carbon, shelter wildlife, filter water, and to provide food, products, and jobs for (2)______ is compromised. Global forest loss also leads to more greenhouse gases in the (3)______. This is much more than what comes from all cars, trucks, trains, planes and ships in the world. But when forests thrive, they absorb carbon dioxide and (4)______ down climate change. Protecting forests does much more than just keep trees standing because forests are the (5)______ of the planet and serve as the home, market and apothecary of people living on it.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “people”.The most appropriate answer to fill the blank is “people”.Personalities – the different qualities of a person’s character that make them different from other people.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When trees are illegally or irresponsibly cut, forest ecosystems are (1)______. Their ability to store carbon, shelter wildlife, filter water, and to provide food, products, and jobs for (2)______ is compromised. Global forest loss also leads to more greenhouse gases in the (3)______. This is much more than what comes from all cars, trucks, trains, planes and ships in the world. But when forests thrive, they absorb carbon dioxide and (4)______ down climate change. Protecting forests does much more than just keep trees standing because forests are the (5)______ of the planet and serve as the home, market and apothecary of people living on it.
questions.explanation: correct answer is “atmosphere”.Hydrosphere – the water on or surrounding the surface of the globe.Atmosphere – the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth or any other star, planet, etc.Thermosphere – the region of the atmosphere above the mesosphere.Microsphere – A microscopic hollow sphere, especially of a protein or synthetic polymer.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When trees are illegally or irresponsibly cut, forest ecosystems are (1)______. Their ability to store carbon, shelter wildlife, filter water, and to provide food, products, and jobs for (2)______ is compromised. Global forest loss also leads to more greenhouse gases in the (3)______. This is much more than what comes from all cars, trucks, trains, planes and ships in the world. But when forests thrive, they absorb carbon dioxide and (4)______ down climate change. Protecting forests does much more than just keep trees standing because forests are the (5)______ of the planet and serve as the home, market and apothecary of people living on it.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is “slow”. Except “slow” remaining option is grammatically incorrect.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When trees are illegally or irresponsibly cut, forest ecosystems are (1)______. Their ability to store carbon, shelter wildlife, filter water, and to provide food, products, and jobs for (2)______ is compromised. Global forest loss also leads to more greenhouse gases in the (3)______. This is much more than what comes from all cars, trucks, trains, planes and ships in the world. But when forests thrive, they absorb carbon dioxide and (4)______ down climate change. Protecting forests does much more than just keep trees standing because forests are the (5)______ of the planet and serve as the home, market and apothecary of people living on it.
